body:
## What

Adds the two files that were missing for the Claude Code GitHub integration:

1. **`.github/workflows/claude-code.yml`** — responds to `@claude` mentions in issues, PR comments, and PR review comments, and runs automatically on opened/updated PRs. Uses `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`.
2. **`CLAUDE.md`** — Claude-specific priorities, required checks, and review standards.

## Notes / deviations from the original guide

- **`AGENTS.md` left untouched.** The repo already has a substantial, repo-specific `AGENTS.md` (19KB). Overwriting it with the generic version from the guide would lose operational knowledge, so `CLAUDE.md` defers to it instead of duplicating it.
- **Commands tailored to the real repo.** This is a Python/FastAPI project using a `Makefile` at the root (not `npm`). `CLAUDE.md` uses the actual targets: `make test`, `make lint`, `make type-check`, `make env-check`, `make security-smoke`, `make doctor`, plus `python scripts/check_env_contract.py` (which exists).
- **Concurrency + timeout** added to the workflow to avoid stacking redundant runs.

## Required before this works

Add this secret under **Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions**:

- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — Anthropic / Claude API key

`APP_ID` / `APP_PRIVATE_KEY` are only needed if you wire up a custom GitHub App instead of the default workflow token.

## Test

- `python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-code.yml'))"` → YAML OK

https://claude.ai/code/session_017Y6R1o9tKLLQFn1az3RF6S

---
_Generated by [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code/session_017Y6R1o9tKLLQFn1az3RF6S)_

commits.messageBody:
- .github/workflows/claude-code.yml: responds to @claude in issues/PRs and
  reviews opened PRs via anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 (needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
- CLAUDE.md: Claude-specific priorities and review standards, tailored to the
  repo's real make/pytest commands; defers to existing AGENTS.md for repo map

https://claude.ai/code/session_017Y6R1o9tKLLQFn1az3RF6S
commits.messageHeadline: Add Claude Code GitHub Action workflow and CLAUDE.md

commits.messageBody:
claude-code-action@v1 fetches an OIDC token during setup and fails with
'Could not fetch an OIDC token' without id-token: write.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017Y6R1o9tKLLQFn1az3RF6S
commits.messageHeadline: Add id-token: write permission to Claude Code workflow

commits.messageBody:
- Trivy: bump aquasecurity/trivy-action from non-existent @0.24.0 to @master
  (consistent with docker-build.yml) so the action resolves.
- Gitleaks: gitleaks-action@v2 needs a paid GITLEAKS_LICENSE for orgs; mark the
  step continue-on-error and wire the secret so it stops failing PRs until the
  license secret is added.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017Y6R1o9tKLLQFn1az3RF6S
commits.messageHeadline: Fix Trivy action version and stop Gitleaks blocking PRs

commits.messageBody:
After bumping trivy-action to @master the scan runs and flags the dummy
literal sk_live_REALDANGEROUSKEYSECRET inside the repo's own secret-detection
scripts (v7_launch_verify.sh, v10_master_verify.sh) as a Stripe key. These are
false positives — the literal is a placeholder in a grep allowlist. Skip only
these two files so Stripe detection stays active everywhere else.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017Y6R1o9tKLLQFn1az3RF6S
commits.messageHeadline: Exclude repo's own secret-scan scripts from Trivy false positives

commits.messageBody:
The previous comment literally contained the dummy Stripe-key placeholder,
which Trivy then flagged on the workflow file itself. Describe it instead.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017Y6R1o9tKLLQFn1az3RF6S
commits.messageHeadline: Reword Trivy skip comment to avoid re-tripping the secret rule

commits.messageBody:
Trivy now runs (after the action version fix) and correctly flags real,
pre-existing next@15.1.3 vulnerabilities in frontend/ and apps/web/ lockfiles
(2 CRITICAL + 8 HIGH). These are not false positives.

Per maintainer decision, the Next.js upgrade (target 15.5.16, which clears all
10 entries) is handled in a dedicated PR to keep this integration PR small and
reviewable. Until then, suppress only these specific CVEs, scoped to the two
affected lockfiles, with an expiry of 2026-09-06 so they cannot be forgotten:
after that date Trivy fails again and forces the upgrade. All other findings
still block CI.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017Y6R1o9tKLLQFn1az3RF6S
commits.messageHeadline: Time-box pre-existing Next.js CVEs in Trivy pending dedicated upgrade

commits.messageBody:
Patches the 2 CRITICAL + 8 HIGH next vulnerabilities Trivy flagged
(CVE-2025-29927 middleware auth bypass, CVE-2025-55182 RSC pre-auth RCE,
and 8 HIGH DoS/SSRF/info-disclosure advisories). 15.5.19 is the latest 15.x
patch and clears all 10 findings while staying within the 15.x line.

- Bump next + eslint-config-next to 15.5.19; regenerate both lockfiles.
- next-env.d.ts auto-updated by 15.5 (typed-routes reference).
- Both apps build cleanly (next build verified locally).
- Drop the temporary .trivyignore.yaml suppression and its workflow wiring;
  the real upgrade supersedes it. The secret-scan skip-files for the repo's
  own verification scripts remain (genuine false positives).

https://claude.ai/code/session_017Y6R1o9tKLLQFn1az3RF6S
commits.messageHeadline: Upgrade Next.js 15.1.3 -> 15.5.19 in frontend and apps/web

title: Add Claude Code GitHub Action workflow and CLAUDE.md
